name: Chocolate Refrigerator Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Blend in the egg.
3. Measure the flour by spooning it into a dry measuring cup and leveling off, or sift it through a fine mesh sieve. Stir the flour, cocoa powder, and salt into the butter mixture; blend well until just combined.
4. Chill the dough for 1 to 2 hours.
5. Press the chilled dough into two long, smooth rectangles about 1 1/2 inches in diameter. Roll each rectangle around chopped pecans, pressing the nuts into all sides.
6. Chill the formed cookies overnight.
7. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius).
8. Cut the cooled cookies into 1/8 inch slices using a sharp knife.
9. Place the cookie slices on an ungreased baking sheet in a single layer.
10. Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly golden brown.
11. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely.
12. Melt the German Sweet Chocolate in a double boiler, then frost the center of each cookie.
This recipe makes about 8 dozen cookies.
